raw_text: On multiple occasions, it felt as though another advertisement arrived far sooner than expected. Whether this was due to a technical issue, differences in how Spotify counts listening time, or another explanation, the result was the same: disappointment. When a company makes a promise, users expect that promise to be honored. If listeners believe they are receiving 30 minutes without interruptions and then encounter another ad much sooner, trust in the platform starts to erode. They are just looking for profit, sad. 🫩✌️

This paired up with the fact that you still have ads just makes Spotify a pointless and unless app for people who don’t want to spend. Now I am a person who typically buys premium when I can, but I am noticing that the prices keep slowly increasing a NOTICEABLE amount (not that the price was ever reasonable). Overall if you don’t have the money to spend, STAY AWAY FROM SPOTIFY; and even if you do—it’s still not that great of an app, you should spend it elsewhere.

Incredibly slow and glitchy, no matter how much I clear my cache. No, I am not going to accept that ‘having too many liked songs’ is a factor as these changes have only happened in the past year or so. STOP letting AI write your code. Stop adding TikTok features - it’s a music app not a scrolling app. It was completely fine the way it used to be. I know that I am not the only user experience this slowness. Jamming it full of useless AI features that no one needs is just ruining the app experience. I have been an avid Spotify premium user for years but I am now considering switching my family to a different platform because the app is now barely functional. I doubt this review will have any effect as developers are obviously more concerned with AI-ing the app as much as possible for some sick reason instead of maintaining the reliable usable app they had before. Just insanity, not to mention the prevalence of AI music allowed on the platform now. Just feels like spitting in the face of everything human .
title: You are ruining this app with AI

raw_text: This kind of thing is excusable in a car type situation, given that they don’t build cars with cassette decks or CD players any more, but I don’t use it anywhere else. I much prefer a solid something in my musical listening. I’m not a vinyl lunatic or anything but I do prefer an object to all this ‘streaming’ business. Perhaps it’s mere fetishisation, yes, for music does indeed live in the ether, the air, but then there are covers, sleeves, etc to be considered. It’s certainly fair to ask ‘Where is music?’ and the answer is clearly ‘Well, quite as much on Spotify, etc as it is on any record or tape (not to mention the radio, of course)’, but still, but still. Beyond which and besides, I’d rather think my favourite artists are getting a goodly lump than some so-called tech-whizz type, to be honest. All of which said, my main gripe is actually with the present day and the future, and by using what some might see as outmoded mechanisms or whatever I can at least try to cling to some vestige of the past, the dying world, or something.

raw_text:
Search has always been bad - but as the offering increases, you’re often stuck with the first tranche of results with no concept of page two. Adding information to the search usually just adds bad results rather reordering good ones to the top.

There’s no real way see information from a long title. Anything too far left is forever invisible. Tables visible on the desktop app aren’t to be found on the mobile app! Why? Then again in the absence of columns sorting and filtering- tables on the desktop are no good if it doesn’t fit on a single screen.

I can’t define a subsection of a long musical piece in a playlist. In opera that often means you’re stuck with 10 minutes surrounding the 3 minute aria or chorus you want.

These all feel one intern week away from implementing. Boo!


In some places locally searching a playlist now works. Good.

raw_text:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

J’utilise Spotify depuis longtemps et c’est de loin la meilleure application de musique que j’ai essayée. L’interface est simple, moderne et très facile à utiliser. J’adore pouvoir découvrir de nouvelles chansons grâce aux playlists personnalisées et aux recommandations qui correspondent vraiment à mes goûts musicaux.

La qualité du son est excellente et il y a un immense choix de musique, de balados et de contenus pour tous les styles. J’aime aussi le fait de pouvoir créer mes propres playlists et les partager avec mes amis. L’application fonctionne très bien et je l’utilise tous les jours, que ce soit à la maison, à l’école, en voiture ou pendant mes activités.

Spotify rend l’écoute de musique agréable et pratique. Les mises à jour apportent régulièrement de nouvelles fonctionnalités intéressantes et l’expérience s’améliore constamment. Je recommande cette application à tous ceux qui aiment la musique. Merci à toute l’équipe Spotify pour cette excellente application !

- I hate how you can’t just single click to like and unlike songs like you used to be able to.
- It’s also been painfully slow lately. Apparently that means I have too many liked songs, so how can I quickly move those songs? 
- It shows how much being able to mass select tracks would be hugely beneficial. 
- and I just found out the owner of this app supports war and weaponry, so my gripes are probably moot. I think I’ll just migrate to Deezer. Supporting music shouldn’t be so ethically and morally difficult.

raw_text: I’ve rated Spotify before and previously gave it 5 stars. However, there is one thing I would like to mention that lowers its rating. The algorithm that recommends music to you is not great. I don’t know if it’s a cost issue or just that the algorithm isn’t great but it will recommend me really random songs from genres that I have never listened to in my life. I’m not against broadening my music taste however it becomes frustrating when you want to listen to a specific music genre and all it recommends are genres you have no care for. All that being said it is a genuinely good app and despite popular opinion, I personally love their Spotify wrapped. I recommend people to download this app because it genuinely is worth it. If you do not have Spotify premium the adds can be bothersome however there are other options like a family plan that are cheaper than Spotify premium that get rid of the ads. Overall it’s a great app and a worthwhile investment!!

raw_text:
Honestly i think that this is an alright app for a music app with some great features, great cross device support, great accessory support, great social features, and an average user interface experience that could be better.

However, The premium membership is definitely a bit pricey as a premium subscriber myself as it currently starts at just AUD$15.99 per month with a terrible mobile experience for non-subscribers on top of shoving a ton of ads repeatedly that can’t be skipped every few songs down your throat while also not allowing you to upgrade or to even see how much each premium plan costs each month inside the app which is a shame.

raw_text:
Spotify is good if you want to pay the overpriced monthly subscription. Otherwise, you can forget about choosing what songs to listen to and listening to your own playlists/albums without spotify deciding for you what you want to listen to. 

Without premium, you can’t just search for and play a song you want to listen to. It will force you to listen to random reccomended songs first, then a few bunches of 1:30 of unskippable ads, and then finally if you’re lucky it will play you the song you actually searched for. 
Without premium, if you make a playlist and want to listen to it, spotify forces its own “recommended similar songs” into your queue which are often more commonly played instead of the songs that are actually IN THE PLAYLIST YOU CREATED.
Too bad if you want to listen to an album, because spotify will instead choose other songs from other albums from the artist when you click play and then also sprinkle in some songs from entirely different artists and suddenly you’re an hour into listening and all you have heard is 20 minutes of ads, 2 album songs, 3 songs from different albums, and 6 songs from completely different artists.
Also, too bad if you want to skip the songs you didn’t ask for, because there is a limit on the amount of skips you can do per hour too. 
Spotify free is only okay if you use desktop pc or laptop, in which these issues are non-existent.

raw_text:
I’ve never had any complaints about Spotify, until the last few weeks. 

It is now regularly stopping playing music. It says, “We’re having trouble playing this song. Try going online…”

It happens even when I’m online.

If I kill the app and restart it, the same song that was failing will pick up exactly where it left off. I can also start playing a new playlist.

Having this happen when I’m driving means I can’t play music until I can safely pull over and restart the app. It doesn’t matter if I’m using CarPlay or not, it happens either way. 

It happens to everybody in my family, as well. There’s no explanation that makes sense, except for a new bug being introduced that’s impacting playback.
clean_text:

raw_text:
Spotify plays podcasts again right after finishing them. 
There is no option of blocking content/creators (even temporarily)and podcasts/content I have no interest anymore appear in the auto-feed which cannot be influenced.
No private mode in the app - when kids play their music (which might not be available in the Spotify kids version) it affects recommendations and playlists created by Spotify.

The issues above have been addressed online for years with Spotify not reacting or suggesting a complete account reset.

This is poor behalf and maintenance on Spotify‘s behalf and a switch to another provider might be the only alternative.

raw_text:
STEPS: 
When searching inside a playlist (pull down from top) the initial search bar shows one line down from the header. 
BUT On tap it jumps one line above…. 😡
Often with a delay…leading to a second tap to initiate search. 
ISSUE: Results in accidentally selecting the first search result in the list 😞
Ruins your currently playing playlist selecting the wrong track on the new playlist. Ruining playing song for audience. 

Solutions:
- Search shows in the top line on pull down (same line as back)
- Or search stays on the second line (same place) the whole time. 
- Or start search result list one line down so one can’t select the first track by accident. 
- fix delay. Delay has been improved (worse on older devices) but still not good when searching your playlists to queue tracks for an audience.

raw_text: Spotify used to be great and then recently it’s started to be so ridiculously slow, I’ve tried turning off extra features like Canvas and Smart Shuffle, removed downloads, cleared cache, dropped audio and visual quality, checked stuff with my own phone and it’s still SO slow! Checked it across multiple devices too and the same thing! This started a few months ago and seems to be a problem with every person I’ve talked to who uses Spotify as well. Just so many unnecessary features that slow down the app with no way to turn them off: concerts, merch, DJ, song DNA etc. At what point do you add so many features that it ruins the basic function of the app? Why can I load all of these extra features but can’t see the tracklist of an album or play a song? Just ridiculous.

Goodbye Spotify, I’m cancelling my subscription. I received an email today informing me of a $2 price increase per month. Well that’s the final nail in the coffin. I’m not paying extra for a service that isn’t fit for purpose. Your podcast queue feature has been broken for some time. Queues will suddenly disappear and random episodes are added to the end of any queue I create. So instead of the queue finishing, it continues with episodes I don’t want. Also, episodes listened to are no longer deleting automatically despite having that option selected. 
The measly amount of audiobook hours isn’t worth paying premium for either. Goodbye Spotify, It’s sad to see a great service in decline.

raw_text:
Brawl Stars is more than just a mobile game—it is a complete, polished, and deeply engaging experience that represents what mobile gaming can be at its very best. From its fast-paced action and unique characters to its constant updates and strong sense of community, Brawl Stars has proven itself to be a standout title in the crowded world of mobile games. Developed by Supercell, a company already known for successful games like Clash of Clans and Clash Royale, Brawl Stars builds upon that legacy and pushes it even further. The game deserves a full five-star rating not only because it is fun, but because it consistently delivers quality, fairness, creativity, and long-term enjoyment.
At its core, Brawl Stars is a multiplayer action game that focuses on short, intense battles. Matches are quick, usually lasting only a few minutes, which makes the game perfect for both short play sessions and longer gaming marathons. This design choice is one of the game’s greatest strengths. Players never feel like they are wasting time, and every match feels meaningful. Whether you win or lose, you gain experience, learn new strategies, and improve your skills. This constant sense of progression keeps players motivated and engaged.
One of the most impressive aspects of Brawl Stars is its wide and diverse roster of characters, known as brawlers. Each brawler has a unique personality, attack style, super ability, and role on the battlefield. Some brawlers specialize in close-range combat, while others excel at long-range attacks, area control, healing, or support. This variety allows players to find characters that match their preferred playstyle. It also encourages experimentation, as trying new brawlers can completely change how the game feels. Unlocking new brawlers is exciting and rewarding, and mastering them provides a strong sense of accomplishment.
The depth of gameplay in Brawl Stars is another reason it stands out. While the controls are simple and easy to learn, the strategies involved can be surprisingly complex. Positioning, timing, map awareness, teamwork, and decision-making all play important roles in determining the outcome of a match. Skilled players learn when to attack, when to retreat, and how to work together with teammates to control objectives. This balance between accessibility and depth makes Brawl Stars appealing to players of all skill levels, from beginners to competitive veterans.
Game modes are a major highlight of Brawl Stars. The game offers a wide range of modes, each with its own objectives and strategies. Gem Grab focuses on collecting and holding gems, requiring teamwork and coordination. Showdown, available in solo and duo formats, is a survival-based mode that emphasizes individual skill and smart decision-making. Brawl Ball combines action with sports-inspired gameplay, creating fast and chaotic matches that are incredibly fun. Other modes like Heist, Hot Zone, Knockout, and special limited-time events ensure that there is always something new and exciting to play. The rotating nature of these modes keeps the game fresh and prevents it from becoming repetitive.
The maps in Brawl Stars are thoughtfully designed and play a crucial role in gameplay. Each map has its own layout, featuring walls, bushes, open areas, and choke points that influence how matches unfold. Different brawlers perform better on different maps, which adds another layer of strategy. Learning map layouts and understanding which brawlers work best in each situation is part of what makes the game so engaging. The frequent rotation of maps ensures that players must constantly adapt and refine their strategies.
Visually, Brawl Stars is vibrant, colorful, and full of personality. The art style is cartoonish yet polished, making the game appealing to players of all ages. Each brawler is visually distinct, with expressive animations and creative designs that reflect their abilities and personalities. The environments are equally well-designed, with bright colors and clear visual cues that make gameplay easy to follow even during intense moments. The visual clarity is especially important in a fast-paced game like Brawl Stars, and Supercell has done an excellent job in this area.
Sound design and music also contribute significantly to the overall experience. Each brawler has unique sound effects and voice lines that add charm and character. The background music fits the game’s energetic tone and changes depending on events and seasons, helping to keep the experience fresh. These audio elements may seem small, but they play an important role in making the game feel alive and immersive.
One of the most important reasons Brawl Stars deserves a five-star rating is its fair and well-balanced progression system. Many mobile games rely heavily on pay-to-win mechanics, but Brawl Stars avoids this pitfall. While players can spend money to speed up progression or unlock cosmetic items, skill and strategy remain the most important factors in winning matches. Free-to-play players can still enjoy the full game experience, unlock brawlers, and compete at high levels without feeling disadvantaged. This fairness builds trust between the developers and the player base.
The Brawl Pass system is a great example of thoughtful game design. It provides a clear progression path with rewards that feel meaningful and satisfying. Both free and paid tracks offer valuable items, ensuring that all players benefit from playing regularly. Completing quests and challenges feels rewarding without being overly demanding, striking a healthy balance between effort and reward. This system encourages consistent play while respecting players’ time.
Customization is another strong aspect of Brawl Stars. Players can personalize their experience with skins, pins, sprays, and other cosmetic items. These customizations allow players to express themselves and show off their favorite brawlers without affecting gameplay balance. Many skins are creative,

raw_text:
“Enjoy your 30 minutes of ad free music”… ads then continue to play after every song, sometimes not even playing the last 5 seconds of the song before disrupting it with an ad. 

Spotify acts as if it specifically doesn’t want their users to have an enjoyable listening experience, As you can not pick the specific song you want to listen to, even if you play a 10 hour playlist that has that same song repeated for that 10 hour duration, they detect you’re attempting to listen to a song YOU want to listen to, so they play a song of a complete different genre by a complete different artist.

It’s clear this is a money hungry app that doesn’t have much consideration at all for users not willing/can’t afford a monthly/weekly subscription.

If this is what you’re after then use this app. 

Also, If you are excited for your  “Spotify wrapped” just know it’s likely to be inaccurate..

raw_text:
The app isn’t bad, but they should stop recommending rubbish I’m not interested in, like reggaeton or mainstream stuff that only people without a brain care about, because if I listen to metal, for example, I only want to see things related to it or within the rock genre.
They should also add the song’s duration next to each track, so you don’t have to check the player – it should just pop up automatically next to the three dots. It’s not that hard.
And sync up the lyrics too – it’s not that hard. If I could, I’d do it myself. Don’t be useless – we’re paying for a reason.

I thought the music video / podcast update would be so good, I was so wrong. First of all the music videos take ages to load and are usually low quality. Second of all the podcast videos get recommended on your homepage with clickbait thumbnails worse than YouTube, and if you like listening to a few specific true time podcasts all they’ll recommend is horror videos and even innapropriate titled videos with creepy thumbnails.
I go onto Spotify to listen to music at night and I’m met with horrible thumbnails, I don’t know how it takes over my page considering I click the NOT INTERESTED button everytime, clearly doesn’t seem to work. Even podcasts I’ve listened to once keep getting recommended on repeat and I didn’t like them that’s why I listened once, I don’t follow these podcasts and press not interested and they STILL get recommended. Just because I like some true crime girlies doesn’t mean I want horrific recommendations. I pressed follow on podcasts I am interested in and not interested on podcasts I don’t like and would honestly block if I could. What’s the point in these buttons if they don’t work? Honestly it’s ruined my entire Spotify experience not to mention it takes up more energy and storage on my phone over an update that literally IS SO ANNOYING. I want to play music and listen to podcasts. This update is literally taking away from my music experience. AND IM PAYING FOR IT? No thanks I hope Spotify can fix these issues soon because I was a big fan of the app.

Spotify has completely changed the way I experience music. raw_text:
Yes, Spotify is a great app and all that. I love the songs and the playlists, but the ads…? The ads are just wayyy too much, I listen to 1 song and boom, I get hit with the 5 ads, and it is so annoying! It always happens like cmon let me study in peace without so many ads. Yes, having ads is no problem, but, Spotify, you need to be more realistic. No one wants to listen to 1 song, listen to 3 ads, listen to 1 song again, then listen to 5 ads. Hello?! It’s insane, really.

But at least Spotify is better than Apple Music and other music apps. It’s really just the ads that bothers me. I have to listen to the same d for so many times and it lowkey really annoying… It’s also insane how we need premium to loop a song, and sometimes if you don’t have premium, you can’t listen to the same song that u searched up. A different song pops up and ITS SO ANNOYING. Let me listen to the song I want, Spotify!

Overall, the songs are great, but Spotify, please just change your ads. I don’t nest to get hit either 7 ads which are 30 seconds long again, please, Spotify.

title: Why spotify

raw_text:
Music > Following is excluding new releases from artists I follow now.  

Has become painfully slow recently. Takes up to 20 seconds before music loads and starts playing if you haven’t been using the app or it’s been paused too long. 

Spotify needs to update their features and transparency with AI music. Spotify has become flooded with AI generated music and fake bands and does not flag or notify users, or give them an option to block generated music. 

The smart shuffle feature can be a great way to find new songs but functions strangely by playing a predetermined selection of songs depending on which song you start with in your playlist. So it doesn’t actually shuffle randomly. It also seems to only shuffle from certain sections depending on where you start it in a playlist if you have a lot of songs in the playlist like your Liked Songs playlist. Sponsored recommendations are awful and borderline paid for ads from artists trying to get noticed. Regardless of your tastes or genres they will get pushed in your face based on your country and will not be to your tastes at all. I get better artist recommendations on Spotify in Instagram ads which is a joke. The AI DJ is…yeah. Just no. There is no intelligence there at all. Just lazily pushing genres and top artists based on your location again instead of finding you new music based on your actual tastes and listening history. Gave up trying to train it and repeatedly hiding the same artists and songs I don’t like.

Sure, without the dash:

Spotify feels less like a music app and more like a memory box. Every playlist holds a version of me: road trips, evening walks, happy days, heartbreaks, and moments when I simply sat staring at the sky. The best thing about Spotify isn’t the millions of songs; it’s how easily it finds the soundtrack for whatever I’m feeling. raw_text:
Was revolutionary when it came out and don't get me wrong, i may own my music and host them on my private server, but having Spotify for whenever i travel outside is a great alternative. No buggy issues and what not. App is almost splendid in design and UI but it has way better and more features in TV os variants. I am also happy to know as I listen, my favorite indie musicians gain some money. My close friend even has his music on Spotify too. However here are my critiques:

The ads have increased too much. Yes i refuse to pay money, what with this economy lately. It is especially frustrating when there's multiple ads playing one after another before the music returns. Come on, relax a bit. As is now, i sometimes just entirely close out of Spotify when it has the audacity to play more than one ad in a row. Super not a fan of that. If it just played ONE ad for a few seconds then i would be 100% ok with it and not get annoyed. And also, if the devs listen to that suggestion, that does not mean to then increase ad frequency either. Every few songs is better and doesn't leave a sour taste in my mouth. After all if i listen to music then it very likely means I'll be at it for hours, which is plenty of ads to fit in there for Spotify to make a profit. Don't cram so many ads as it is currently else I'm just switching out the app and listening to offline downloads on a different app, or I'll just use YouTube on my phone (no ads there for my use case scenario).

raw_text: This app runs terribly on my iPhone SE 1st gen, so many bugs and crashes. Several UI elements are just broken, e.g. when sorting a podcast playlist, all the sorting options are invisible. And Spotify recently pushed a server-side update that shows lyrics on the now playing screen, right beneath the album cover art. There is supposed to be a button to turn it off, but since it's server-side, with no client updates on older iOS, it is literally impossible to turn off, and it kinda breaks the UI on the iPhone SE since the screen is literally too small to display everything properly. The continued server-side adding of features to old iOS clients is just a pretty bad experience. The app also runs horribly and keeps crashing, and it only got worse after they added the lyrics to the 'now playing' screen. Oh and on top of it all, Spotify constantly pesters me to join other people's jams just because I'm connected to a very large network at the dorm I'm living at. And the chance of accidentally joining rather than dismissing is way too high. And it's just really annoying constantly being interrupted and asked to join random strangers' sessions. Again, no way to turn that off either.

And Spotify recently pushed a server-side update that shows lyrics on the now playing screen, right beneath the album cover art. There is supposed to be a button to turn it off, but since it's server-side, with no client updates on older iOS, it is literally impossible to turn off, and it kinda breaks the UI on the iPhone SE since the screen is literally too small to display everything properly. The continued server-side adding of features to old iOS clients is just a pretty bad experience. The app also runs horribly and keeps crashing, and it only got worse after they added the lyrics to the 'now playing' screen. Oh and on top of it all, Spotify constantly pesters me to join other people's jams just because I'm connected to a very large network at the dorm I'm living at. And the chance of accidentally joining rather than dismissing is way too high. And it's just really annoying constantly being interrupted and asked to join random strangers' sessions. Again, no way to turn that off either.
title: No support for older iOS

raw_text: Once again, after another year, I revisited the options of YouTube Music, Apple Music and Amazon Music. Alexa do not support YouTube. Spotify stays the best.
